error.signature: Error: init mode failed (unable to connect to the target)
error.regex: (unable to connect to the target|init mode failed|Error connecting.*ST-?LINK|No STM32 target found)

verdict.summary: ST-LINK cannot connect to STM32 via SWD/JTAG. Wiring, BOOT pins, sleep mode, or flash protection.

dead_ends.action: Increase SWD clock frequency for stable connection
dead_ends.why_fails: Higher frequency makes marginal connections WORSE; long wires need LOWER frequencies

dead_ends.action: Replace the ST-LINK adapter assuming it is broken
dead_ends.why_fails: Usually the target chip or wiring is the problem. Verify with a second target board first.

dead_ends.action: Flash via serial bootloader to fix debug port
dead_ends.why_fails: Serial flash may work but does not re-enable SWD if firmware disabled the SWD pins

workarounds.action: Check SWD wiring: SWDIO, SWCLK, GND, and NRST
workarounds.how: Minimum: GND, SWDIO (PA13), SWCLK (PA14). Keep wires under 10cm.

workarounds.action: Erase flash via BOOT0 pin to recover bricked chips
workarounds.how: Set BOOT0=HIGH, power cycle, erase via STM32CubeProgrammer UART, then BOOT0=LOW
